traditions.pageSubtitle: Discover the Easter game that has united generations of families across the Christian world.

sections.title: Why Do We Crack Eggs?
paragraphs: The tradition of cracking Easter eggs is one of the oldest Christian customs. Legend says that Saint Mary Magdalene went to the Roman Emperor Tiberius, offered him an egg and declared "Christ is Risen!". The Emperor did not believe her, saying a dead man could no more rise than the white egg in her hand could turn red — and at that very moment, the egg turned crimson.

The red color symbolizes the blood shed by Christ on the cross, while the cracking of the egg represents the opening of the Holy Tomb. Today, this tradition is a joyful reason for families to gather around the Easter table, whether Orthodox or Catholic.

sections.title: The Rules of the Game
paragraphs: There is a simple protocol, passed down through generations, for how Easter eggs should be cracked:

rules.label: Top to Top
rules.desc: The rule for Easter Sunday — always start with the pointy ends.

rules.label: Top to Bottom
rules.desc: Allowed from the second day onwards.

rules.label: Bottom to Bottom
rules.desc: Reserved strictly for the third day of celebration.

sections.highlight: The striker says "Christ is Risen!" and the other responds "Indeed He is Risen!" or simply "Happy Easter!"

sections.title: The Meaning of Colors
paragraphs: While red is the most iconic Easter egg color, other colors each carry their own meaning:

colors.label: RED
colors.desc: Symbol of Christ's sacrifice, divine love and life.

colors.label: YELLOW
colors.desc: Represents the light of the Resurrection and the warmth of spring.

colors.label: BLUE
colors.desc: Symbolizes the sky, faith and the infinite spiritual world.

colors.label: GREEN
colors.desc: The color of spring, renewal and the hope of a new beginning.

sections.title: How to Pick the Strongest Egg
paragraphs: The secret to winning an egg duel lies in picking the right egg from the basket:
tips: Look for an egg with a pointier tip — force concentrates better on a smaller surface.
Tap it lightly against your teeth — a dense, sharp sound means a thick, tough shell.
Squeeze it gently in your hand — it should feel firm, with no hidden micro-cracks under the paint.
Grandmothers say: hens that eat more calcium lay eggs with thicker shells!

sections.title: Easter Superstitions
paragraphs: The egg cracking game is not just a competition — it's tied to beliefs about luck and health:
tips: Whoever has the egg that survives all duels at the table will be healthy and strong all year.
The first red egg is kept behind the icon until next Easter — it protects the home from misfortune.
If you find an egg with two yolks, double luck awaits you this year.
The shells of Easter eggs are never thrown away — tradition asks you to bury them in the garden for a good harvest.

faq.q: Why do we crack eggs at Easter?
faq.a: The tradition is one of the oldest Christian customs. The red color symbolizes Christ's blood, and the cracking of the egg represents the opening of the Holy Tomb. Legend ties it to Mary Magdalene's encounter with Emperor Tiberius.

faq.q: What are the rules for cracking eggs?
faq.a: Start top-to-top on Easter Sunday. Top-to-bottom from the second day onwards. The striker says 'Christ is Risen!' and the other responds 'Indeed He is Risen!'

faq.q: What do the egg colors mean?
faq.a: Red — symbol of Christ's blood. Yellow — light and abundance. Blue — sky and faith. Green — spring and hope.

faq.q: How do I pick the strongest egg?
faq.a: Look for a pointy tip, tap it lightly against your teeth for a dense sound, and squeeze it gently — it should feel firm, with no hidden cracks.
The surviving egg brings health. The first red egg is kept behind the icon. Shells are buried in the garden for a good harvest.

dyeing.pageSubtitle: Bright colors from natural ingredients, just as grandmothers have done for centuries.

sections.title: Traditional Red (Onion Skins)
paragraphs: The oldest and most widespread method. The deep red from onion skins is the color of an authentic Easter. Tradition says the first egg should be dyed red on Holy Thursday, with a wax cross drawn on it before dipping — that cross protects the home.
steps: Collect the dry skins from 10–15 onions and boil them in 2 liters of water with 2 tablespoons of white vinegar for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid and let it cool slightly.
Immerse the raw eggs (washed beforehand) and simmer gently for 10–12 minutes.
Take them out and let them dry. For shine, rub a tiny bit of oil over each egg with a soft cloth.

sections.title: Golden Yellow (Turmeric)
paragraphs: A warm, bright yellow — simple and spectacular.
steps: Dissolve 3–4 tablespoons of turmeric powder in 3 cups of hot water.
Add 2 tablespoons of white vinegar (it fixes the color better).
Immerse boiled, cooled eggs and leave for 2–3 hours. For deep yellow, leave overnight in the fridge.

sections.title: Pastel Blue (Red Cabbage)
paragraphs: Surprisingly, red cabbage produces blue! The method is slow, but the result is magical.
steps: Chop half a red cabbage and boil it in 4 cups of water for 45 minutes.
Remove the cabbage and add 2–3 tablespoons of vinegar to the purple-blue water.
Immerse boiled eggs and leave them for several hours (4–8) or overnight.

sections.title: Important Tips
tips: Always use vinegar — it fixes the color and prevents cracking.
White eggs absorb color much better than brown ones.
Let the eggs reach room temperature 30 minutes before boiling — it prevents cracking.
For patterns, press parsley leaves onto the egg and wrap in an old stocking before dyeing!

calendar.pageSubtitle: Find out when Easter falls between {startYear} and {endYear}
howParagraphs: Ever wondered why Easter falls on a different date each year, unlike Christmas? The rule was set at the Council of Nicaea in AD 325.
Easter is always celebrated on the first Sunday after the first full moon that falls on or after the spring equinox (March 21).

The difference between Orthodox and Catholic Easter comes from the different calendars used. The Catholic Church uses the Gregorian calendar, while the Orthodox Church still calculates Easter using the older Julian calendar.

guide.pageSubtitle: Everything you need to know to crack Easter eggs online.

steps.title: Pick a Nickname
steps.desc: Go to the homepage and type a nickname between 3 and 21 characters. It will show up in the leaderboard and game rooms.
steps.tip: Pick something memorable — your opponents will see it!

steps.title: Customize Your Egg
steps.desc: Tap your egg to choose a color: Red, Blue, Gold or Green. Each color carries a unique traditional pattern.
steps.tip: Color doesn't affect your odds — it's purely aesthetic.

steps.title: Pick a Game Mode
steps.desc: You have three options: Play with a Friend (private room with code), Play with Anyone (public arena), or create a Group for multiple players.

steps.title: Crack!
steps.desc: When both players are in the room, the crack happens automatically. One egg breaks, the other survives. The result is random, like in real life!
steps.tip: The striker says 'Christ is Risen!' and the other responds 'Indeed He is Risen!'

steps.title: Climb the Leaderboard
steps.desc: Every win counts! Your wins are automatically added to the global leaderboard.

steps.title: Unlock Achievements
steps.desc: The game has 16 achievements to unlock — from your first win to 1000 wins. Check them on your profile page!
steps.tip: Some achievements are secret — discover them yourself!

faq.a: Yes, completely free. No ads, no in-app purchases.

faq.q: Do I need to create an account?
faq.a: No. Pick a nickname and start playing instantly. Your stats are saved in your browser.

faq.q: Can I play on my phone?
faq.a: Yes! It works on any phone, tablet or computer with a browser. You can even install it as an app.

faq.q: How do I invite a friend?
faq.a: Tap 'Play with a Friend', create a room and send the 4-character code to your friend.
Groups let you create a team with friends or family. You can challenge group members directly to duels.
Every win adds a point to your personal score and to the global leaderboard. Top 10 players are displayed.

despre.pageSubtitle: The story behind the traditional Easter game, moved online.

sections.title: Our Mission
paragraphs: Trosc.fun was born from a simple idea: bring the tradition of Easter egg cracking into the digital age, without losing its charm.
Whether you're far from family or just want to have fun with friends in a new way, we bring you the authentic Easter egg battle experience — directly in your browser, on any device.

sections.title: How It Works
paragraphs: Every game simulates a real egg duel:
list: Pick a nickname and customize your egg (color).
Create a private room or enter the Arena to find an opponent.
The eggs crack in real time — one breaks, the other survives.
Your wins climb the global leaderboard.

sections.title: Tradition & Technology
paragraphs: We blend tradition with modern technology:

features.label: PWA
features.desc: Installable on your phone as an app

sections.title: 1. Service Description
sections.body: Trosc.fun is a free online game that simulates the traditional Easter egg cracking custom. It's available through any browser, without installation, on any device.

sections.title: 2. Use of Service
sections.body:
• The game is free and does not require registration with real personal data.
  • You pick a nickname to play with. It must not contain vulgar or offensive language.
  • Using the game for harassment, spam or any malicious activity is forbidden.

sections.title: 3. User-Generated Content
sections.body: Chat messages and nicknames are user-generated content. We reserve the right to automatically filter vulgar content and restrict access for users who break the rules.

sections.title: 4. Intellectual Property
sections.body: The design, source code, graphics and content of Trosc.fun are the property of the Trosc.fun team. The Easter traditions described belong to shared cultural heritage.

sections.title: 5. Limitation of Liability
sections.body: Trosc.fun is provided "as is". We don't guarantee permanent availability of the service. Game outcomes are random (50/50) and don't involve real winnings.

sections.title: 6. Changes
sections.body: We reserve the right to modify these terms. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

sections.title: 7. Contact
sections.body: For questions, write to us at ciocnim@mail.com.

sections.title: 1. What Data We Collect
sections.body:
Trosc.fun is a free Easter game. We collect the bare minimum of data, strictly necessary for the game to work:

  • Nickname — chosen by you, stored locally and on the server for leaderboards.
  • Game stats — wins, losses, achievements.
  • Anonymous identifier — a randomly generated ID for counting online users (contains no personal data).

sections.title: 2. How We Use the Data
sections.body:
Data is used exclusively for:

  • Running the real-time multiplayer game.
  • Displaying leaderboards and achievements.
  • Counting online users.

sections.title: 3. Data Storage
sections.body: Local data (nickname, settings, theme) is saved in your browser's localStorage. Leaderboard data is stored on secure servers (Upstash Redis) with TTL (automatic expiration).

sections.title: 4. Cookies and Similar Technologies
sections.body: Trosc.fun uses localStorage to save your settings (theme, nickname, stats). We don't use tracking cookies or third-party analytics.

sections.title: 5. Data Sharing
sections.body: We don't sell, share or transmit personal data to third parties. The services we use (Pusher for WebSocket, Vercel for hosting) process technical data according to their own privacy policies.

sections.title: 6. Your Rights (GDPR)
sections.body:
Under GDPR, you have the right to:

  • Access your data — see your profile in the app.
  • Delete data — clear localStorage from your browser or contact us.
  • Rectify data — change your nickname at any time.

sections.title: 7. Contact
sections.body: For privacy questions, write to us at ciocnim@mail.com.
